lines.gurmukhi: ਕਉਨੁ ਮਰੈ ਕੋ ਦੇਇ ਸੰਤਾਪੋ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kaun marai ko dee santaapo |1|
lines.english: Who dies? Who inflicts pain? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਉਨੁ ਕੋ ਪੂਤੁ ਪਿਤਾ ਕੋ ਕਾ ਕੋ ॥
lines.translit: kaun ko poot pitaa ko kaa ko |
lines.english: Whose son is he? Whose father is he?

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਠਗ ਜਗ ਕਉ ਠਗਉਰੀ ਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: har tthag jag kau tthgauree laaee |
lines.english: The Lord is the thug, who has drugged and robbed the whole world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਆ ਤਤ ਲੇਹੁ ਸਰੀਰ ਬਿਚਾਰੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: eaa tat lehu sareer bichaaree |2|
lines.english: Contemplate this reality within your body. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਬਿਓਗ ਕੈਸੇ ਜੀਅਉ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har ke biog kaise jeeo meree maaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am separated from the Lord; how can I survive, O my mother? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਉਨ ਕੋ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਕਉਨ ਕੀ ਨਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaun ko purakh kaun kee naaree |
lines.english: Whose husband is he? Whose wife is she?

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਈ ਠਗਉਰੀ ਠਗੁ ਪਹਿਚਾਨਿਆ ॥੩॥੩੯॥
lines.translit: gee tthgauree tthag pahichaaniaa |3|39|
lines.english: The effects of the drug have vanished, since I recognized the thug. ||3||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਠਗ ਸਿਉ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer tthag siau man maaniaa |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, my mind is pleased and satisfied with the thug.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਕਟਿ ਪਰਮ ਗਤਿ ਪਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: janam maran katt param gat paaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I have cut away birth and death, and attained the supreme status. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਬ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਭਏ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਮ ਸਹਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ab mo kau bhe raajaa raam sahaaee |
lines.english: Now, the Lord, my King, has become my help and support.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਉ ਜਪੁ ਰਸਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: amrit naam jpau jap rasanaa |
lines.english: I chant with my tongue and meditate on the Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧੂ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਦੀਓ ਰਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: saadhoo sangat deeo ralaae |
lines.english: He has united me with the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਦੂਤ ਤੇ ਲੀਓ ਛਡਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: panch doot te leeo chhaddaae |
lines.english: He has rescued me from the five demons.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਮੋਲ ਦਾਸੁ ਕਰਿ ਲੀਨੋ ਅਪਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: amol daas kar leeno apanaa |1|
lines.english: He has made me his own slave. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਨ ਕਮਲ ਸਿਉ ਲਾਗੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: charan kamal siau laagee preet |
lines.english: I have fallen in love with His Lotus Feet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀਨੋ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: satigur keeno praupakaar |
lines.english: The True Guru has blessed me with His generosity.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਢਿ ਲੀਨ ਸਾਗਰ ਸੰਸਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: kaadt leen saagar sansaar |
lines.english: He has lifted me up, out of the world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਬਿੰਦੁ ਬਸੈ ਨਿਤਾ ਨਿਤ ਚੀਤ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gobind basai nitaa nit cheet |2|
lines.english: The Lord of the Universe dwells continually within my consciousness. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਤਪਤਿ ਬੁਝਿਆ ਅੰਗਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa tapat bujhiaa angiaar |
lines.english: The burning fire of Maya has been extinguished.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਸੰਤੋਖੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਆਧਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: man santokh naam aadhaar |
lines.english: My mind is contented with the Support of the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲਿ ਥਲਿ ਪੂਰਿ ਰਹੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸੁਆਮੀ ॥
lines.translit: jal thal poor rahe prabh suaamee |
lines.english: God, the Lord and Master, is totally permeating the water and the land.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਤ ਪੇਖਉ ਤਤ ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jat pekhau tat antarajaamee |3|
lines.english: Wherever I look, there is the Inner-knower, the Searcher of hearts. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬ ਲਿਖਤੁ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਮੇਰੇ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: poorab likhat miliaa mere bhaaee |
lines.english: By pre-ordained destiny, one meets Him, O my Siblings of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਆਪ ਹੀ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: apanee bhagat aap hee drirraaee |
lines.english: He Himself has implanted His devotional worship within me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਪੂਰਨ ਸਾਜ ॥
lines.translit: jis kripaa kare tis pooran saaj |
lines.english: When He grants His Grace, one is perfectly fulfilled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਕੋ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਗਰੀਬ ਨਿਵਾਜ ॥੪॥੪੦॥
lines.translit: kabeer ko suaamee gareeb nivaaj |4|40|
lines.english: Kabeer's Lord and Master is the Cherisher of the poor. ||4||40||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲਿ ਹੈ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਥਲਿ ਹੈ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਸੂਤਕ ਓਪਤਿ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jal hai sootak thal hai sootak sootak opat hoee |
lines.english: There is pollution in the water, and pollution on the land; whatever is born is polluted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮੇ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਮੂਏ ਫੁਨਿ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਸੂਤਕ ਪਰਜ ਬਿਗੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: janame sootak mooe fun sootak sootak paraj bigoee |1|
lines.english: There is pollution in birth, and more pollution in death; all beings are ruined by pollution. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਰੇ ਪੰਡੀਆ ਕਉਨ ਪਵੀਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: kahu re panddeea kaun paveetaa |
lines.english: Tell me, O Pandit, O religious scholar: who is clean and pure?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੈਨਹੁ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਬੈਨਹੁ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਸ੍ਰਵਨੀ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: nainahu sootak bainahu sootak sootak sravanee hoee |
lines.english: There is pollution in the eyes, and pollution in speech; there is pollution in the ears as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਠਤ ਬੈਠਤ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਪਰੈ ਰਸੋਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aootthat baitthat sootak laagai sootak parai rasoee |2|
lines.english: Standing up and sitting down, one is polluted; one's kitchen is polluted as well. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸਾ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਜਪਹੁ ਮੇਰੇ ਮੀਤਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aisaa giaan japahu mere meetaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meditate on such spiritual wisdom, O my friend. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਾਸਨ ਕੀ ਬਿਧਿ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਊ ਜਾਨੈ ਛੂਟਨ ਕੀ ਇਕੁ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: faasan kee bidh sabh koaoo jaanai chhoottan kee ik koee |
lines.english: Everyone knows how to be caught, but hardly anyone knows how to escape.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝਗਰਾ ਏਕੁ ਨਿਬੇਰਹੁ ਰਾਮ ॥
lines.translit: jhagaraa ek niberahu raam |
lines.english: Resolve this one conflict for me, O Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: gaurree |
lines.english: Gauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਰਾਮੁ ਰਿਦੈ ਬਿਚਾਰੈ ਸੂਤਕੁ ਤਿਨੈ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੩॥੪੧॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer raam ridai bichaarai sootak tinai na hoee |3|41|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, those who meditate on the Lord within their hearts, are not polluted. ||3||41||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਬਡਾ ਕੈ ਰਾਮਹਿ ਜਾਨਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: raam baddaa kai raameh jaaniaa |1|
lines.english: Is the Lord greater, or one who knows the Lord? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਬਡਾ ਕਿ ਜਾ ਸਉ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man baddaa ki jaa sau man maaniaa |
lines.english: Is this mind greater, or the One to whom the mind is attuned?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤੁਮ ਅਪਨੇ ਜਨ ਸੌ ਕਾਮੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jau tum apane jan sau kaam |1| rahaau |
lines.english: if you require any work from Your humble servant. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਡਾ ਕਿ ਜਾਸੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: brahamaa baddaa ki jaas upaaeaa |
lines.english: Is Brahma greater, or the One who created Him?

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇਦੁ ਬਡਾ ਕਿ ਜਹਾਂ ਤੇ ਆਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bed baddaa ki jahaan te aaeaa |2|
lines.english: Are the Vedas greater, or the One from which they came? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਗਉੜੀ ਚੇਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: raag gaurree chetee |
lines.english: Raag Gauree Chaytee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥੁ ਬਡਾ ਕਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਦਾਸੁ ॥੩॥੪੨॥
lines.translit: teerath baddaa ki har kaa daas |3|42|
lines.english: is the sacred shrine of pilgrimage greater, or the slave of the Lord? ||3||42||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਹਉ ਭਇਆ ਉਦਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer hau bheaa udaas |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I have become depressed;

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖੌ ਭਾਈ ਗੵਾਨ ਕੀ ਆਈ ਆਂਧੀ ॥
lines.translit: dekhau bhaaee gayaan kee aaee aandhee |
lines.english: Behold, O Siblings of Destiny, the storm of spiritual wisdom has come.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੈ ਉਡਾਨੀ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਕੀ ਟਾਟੀ ਰਹੈ ਨ ਮਾਇਆ ਬਾਂਧੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sabhai uddaanee bhram kee ttaattee rahai na maaeaa baandhee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: It has totally blown away the thatched huts of doubt, and torn apart the bonds of Maya. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਚਿਤੇ ਕੀ ਦੁਇ ਥੂਨਿ ਗਿਰਾਨੀ ਮੋਹ ਬਲੇਡਾ ਟੂਟਾ ॥
lines.translit: duchite kee due thoon giraanee moh baleddaa ttoottaa |
lines.english: The two pillars of double-mindedness have fallen, and the beams of emotional attachment have come crashing down.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸਨਾ ਛਾਨਿ ਪਰੀ ਧਰ ਊਪਰਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਭਾਂਡਾ ਫੂਟਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tisanaa chhaan paree dhar aoopar duramat bhaanddaa foottaa |1|
lines.english: The thatched roof of greed has caved in, and the pitcher of evil-mindedness has been broken. ||1||
